purpose: A report card for the government's PLI program: incentive DISBURSAL graded scheme by scheme (letter grades, criteria stated), the IEM approval record graded on the Annual Report 2025-26 series, PIB-credited companies that have invested and/or started production (every claim carrying its PIB release ID), and an independent Exim Bank cross-validation panel. Complements the policy & investment dashboard: that bulletin measured commitment-vs-actual investment; this one measures whether the GOVERNMENT'S OWN MONEY (the incentive) is actually flowing, and names who is producing.
method: PIB releases fetched directly (browser user-agent + redirect-following defeated the 403s) and verified against release text with exact PRIDs; ministry-primary figures from this repo's earlier 9-ministry pass reused where PIB has no newer number; Exim Bank of India's 'India's International Trade and Investment' (May 2026) and CEAT Industry Tracker (June 2026) used as an independent government-institution cross-check. Letter grades are this repo's stated judgment on two axes -- disbursal progress vs. outlay, and delivery of the scheme's own outcome targets -- with the reasoning shown per row.

cross_scheme_aggregates.headline: Rs 28,748 crore disbursed across all 14 PLI sectors as of 31 Dec 2025 -- roughly 15% of the ~Rs 1.91-1.97 lakh crore program outlay, 5+ years after the first schemes launched.
cross_scheme_aggregates.source_correction: The 'as of 31 Dec 2025' aggregate package does NOT come from DPIIT's Year-End Review (PRID 2201280, which carries older 30-Jun-2025 figures) -- it comes from Parliament-answer releases PRID 2230621 (20 Feb 2026) and PRID 2246089/2246085 (27 Mar 2026). PIB itself states the program outlay inconsistently: Rs 1.97 lakh cr in the Year-End Review vs Rs 1.91 lakh cr in PRID 2230621.

grade_criteria: A = incentive flowing at scale AND scheme outcome targets met/exceeded; B = real disbursal or commissioned production, partial delivery; C = scheme operating but disbursal thin or outcome data not public; D = disbursal negligible relative to outlay years into the window, or material beneficiary attrition; F = zero disbursal and implementation stalled. Grades are this repo's judgment -- the evidence column is the checkable part.

disbursal_report_card.disbursed_as_of: ~Mar 2026 (PRID 2246089; LSEM+ITHW2.0 combined -- exceeds the LSEM-only outlay because it spans both schemes)
disbursal_report_card.evidence: The program's flagship by every outcome metric (PRID 2247771, 1 Apr 2026): investment Rs 17,519cr = 250% of target; production Rs 11.02 lakh cr = 136%; exports Rs 6.21 lakh cr = 127%; smartphones India's #1 export item in CY2025 (Rs 2,62,452cr). Docked from A: MeitY's own Parliament submission shows only 2 of 5 domestic-category mobile applicants performing, and 92% of the jobs target.

disbursal_report_card.disbursed_as_of: Oct 2025 (ministry AR figure; PIB's Dec 2025 release reports sales only -- disbursal not PIB-verified)
disbursal_report_card.evidence: Combined pharma-PLI sales Rs 3.19 lakh cr incl. Rs 2.04 lakh cr exports since inception (PRID 2204595); 191 KSMs/DIs/APIs produced in India for the first time; actual investment 237% of commitment. The largest genuine disbursal-to-outlay ratio of any big scheme.

disbursal_report_card.disbursed_as_of: no separate PIB/ministry disbursal figure found
disbursal_report_card.evidence: Penicillin-G made in India again after ~30 years (Kakinada, Rs 1,910cr, Rs 2,700cr/yr import substitution) and Clavulanic Acid (Nalagarh, Rs 450cr) -- PRID 2121425; imports avoided Rs 3,591cr; 26 KSM/API capacities live; sales Rs 2,315cr to Sep 2025; 27 plants commissioned across 15+ named companies (PRID 2010924).

19 greenfield projects commissioned; 44 products incl. linear accelerators, MRI and CT machines made in India for the first time (PRID 2114011); 13 plants named with products (PRID 2010924). FY26 sector FDI fell 40% -- the production story is ahead of the FDI story.

171 approved applications, >Rs 9,200cr invested (PRID 2230621). One of the few schemes actually paying out at scale -- but PIB names not a single beneficiary company (the Britannia/ITC/Nestle names live only on MoFPI's own PDF), an odd opacity for a consumer-facing sector.

Sales >6x FY20 level; exports Rs 21,033cr (PRID 2230621); 42 beneficiaries named incl. 28 MSMEs (Tejas, HFCL, VVDN, Dixon, Nokia, Foxconn, Jabil...); BSNL's indigenous 4G stack deployed. Outcome evidence strong, payout evidence absent from PIB.

Domestic manufacturing 'has commenced' for compressors, motors, copper tubes and LED drivers (PRID 2230621) with 30+ investors named verbatim (Daikin, Voltas, Hindalco, Amber, LG, Blue Star, Dixon, Signify...). 4.5% of outlay disbursed, and DPIIT still publishes no per-company production status.

Rs 52,942cr committed toward a 48 GW target; ~38,500 direct jobs by Jun 2025; Tranche-II awardees named (Reliance, First Solar, Waaree, Tata Power Solar, ReNew, JSW...). Commitment data rich, execution/payout data thin in PIB.
Sector turnover up 7x; 23 provisional beneficiaries (Asteria, Garuda, IdeaForge...). A tiny scheme that broadly did what a tiny scheme could.

disbursal_report_card.disbursed_as_of: ~Mar 2026 (PRID 2246089; up from Rs 1,350.83cr at Dec 2025 -- payouts accelerating)
disbursal_report_card.evidence: ~9% of outlay disbursed 4+ years in; incremental sales Rs 13,126cr by 72 companies in FY26-to-Dec -- far off the Rs 2.31 lakh cr trajectory. 8 OEMs DVA-certified for 94 variants (names not published). Tata Motors and Mahindra credited by PIB with 'massive investments in EV production capacity' (PRID 2115609).

Rs 48cr disbursed = 0.8% of outlay -- and PIB discloses 14 of 58 Round-1 projects WITHDREW, a beneficiary-attrition figure the Ministry's own site never states. Rs 20,000cr invested of Rs 27,106cr committed and 9,000 jobs are real, but the incentive mechanism itself is barely functioning.

disbursal_report_card.disbursed_as_of: Aug 2025 (PRID 2152545 -- paid to exactly 2 companies, unnamed)
disbursal_report_card.evidence: 0.5% of outlay disbursed 4 years in; only 26 of 74 Round-1/2 applicants had commenced production at last ministry count; zero beneficiary names in any PIB release.

disbursal_report_card.disbursed_as_of: 31 Dec 2025 (PRID 2214039 reports no disbursement -- consistent with the Parliamentary Committee's 'none due to non-fulfilment of eligibility conditions')
disbursal_report_card.evidence: Zero incentive disbursed; Ola's 1 GWh (of 40 allocated) still in 'pilot production' since March 2024, 'working toward stabilizing operations for full-scale commercial production' per PIB's own words; one original awardee (Hyundai Global Motors, 20 GWh) exited without the word 'withdrew' ever appearing in a government document.

iem_report_card.evidence: Approval/implementation record (DPIIT Annual Report 2025-26, Appendices III-VI): Part-A approvals 986 (2024) and 702 (to Dec 2025) after the Apr-2025 threshold raise; Part-B implementation ratio broke upward to 82.0% (2024) and 109.9% (2025) -- implemented investment exceeded same-year intentions for the first time, Rs 5.83 lakh cr implemented in 2025 alone. Docked from A: the monthly IEM statistics series is verifiably dead post-NSWS migration, there is still no investment-size band in the published data, and DPIIT publishes no explanation of its own series' dynamics.

companies_in_production.note: Status chips: PRODUCING = PIB explicitly credits commenced production/commissioning; INVESTED = PIB names the company as an approved investor/beneficiary. Where the identity comes from a non-PIB source, it is labeled -- this repo does not silently launder attribution.

producing: Foxconn Hon Hai (first incentive approved, PRID 1885189)
Samsung (PRID 1662096 beneficiary; smartphone exports India's #1 export item CY2025)
by_scheme.gap: Company-wise iPhone export splits (Tata Electronics $26.3bn vs Foxconn $25.6bn) are Business Standard reporting, not PIB.

producing: Micron, Sanand ATMP -- commercial production, PM-inaugurated 28 Feb 2026 (PRID 2233998)
Kaynes Technology, Sanand -- PM-inaugurated 31 Mar 2026, >6.33M chips/day, exporting to a California customer (PRID 2247278)
CG Semi (CG Power + Renesas + Stars) -- pilot line Aug 2025, full facility PM-inaugurated 4 Jul 2026 (PRID 2281125)
invested: Tata Electronics-PSMC Dholera fab (Rs 91,526cr, FSA signed Mar 2025 -- production not yet commenced)

producing: Penicillin-G, Kakinada (PIB names the drug/site; company = Lyfius Pharma/Aurobindo per Aurobindo's own BSE filing -- non-PIB)
Meghmani LLP, Hetero Drugs, Andhra Organics, Centrient, Honour Lab, Sadhana Nitro Chem + 10 more across 27 commissioned plants (PRID 2010924)
by_scheme.gap: Macleods (Rifampicin) never appears in PIB text.

producing: Poly Medicure, Nipro India, Trivitron + 5 more (PRID 2010924; 19 greenfield projects commissioned)
by_scheme.gap: Meril (Vapi) and BPL Medical -- the 29.10.2024 PM-inauguration releases name only locations and products; company identities are ANI/press, not PIB.

producing: Component manufacturing 'has commenced' for compressors, motors, copper tubes, LED drivers (PRID 2230621) -- without naming which of the 85 companies
by_scheme.gap: Mitsubishi Electric never named in PIB.

producing: Ola Cell Technologies -- 1 GWh 'pilot production' since Mar 2024, not yet full commercial scale (PRID 2214039)
invested: Tata Motors & Mahindra ('massive investments in EV production capacity', PRID 2115609)
Reliance New Energy (5+10 GWh across two rounds)
by_scheme.gap: The 8 DVA-certified OEMs and 10 component champions are counted but never named by PIB.
No PIB release credits a specific PLI solar plant with commenced production.

ZERO company names in PIB for all three -- Food Processing's beneficiaries (Britannia, ITC, Nestle et al.) exist only on MoFPI's own PDF; Textiles' two paid companies are unnamed; Steel's participants are unnamed despite 14 of 58 withdrawing.

exim_bank_cross_validation.source: Exim Bank of India (government EXIM financing institution, independent publishing chain from DPIIT/TRADESTAT): 'India's International Trade and Investment' May 2026 + CEAT Industry Tracker June 2026 (eximbankindia.in/useful-economic-data).
findings: Trade deficit FY2025-26: $333.2bn per Exim/MOCI vs $334.3bn computed in this repo from TRADESTAT chapter data -- cross-validated within 0.3%.

One-year sharpening of the re-run's conclusion: FY26 exports grew just +0.9% ($437.7bn -> $441.7bn) while imports grew +7.4% -- the deficit WIDENED 17.5% in a single year ($283.5bn -> $333.2bn). The import-substitution gap is not merely a 5-year artifact; it deteriorated in the latest year.
Non-petroleum exports $387.9bn (+3.6%) -- the ex-oil export engine grows faster than the headline, consistent with the scheme-covered-chapters story.

Bank credit to PLI sectors is accelerating sharply (CEAT tracker, May-26 YoY): Electronics +23.9%, Vehicles +26.4%, Drugs & Pharma +22.6%, Iron & Steel +22.6% -- independent corroboration that domestic (EBLR-priced) credit, not just equity, is funding PLI-sector capacity.
Total FDI including reinvested earnings: $88.3bn Apr-Feb FY26 -- the broader RBI-basis measure alongside DPIIT's $58.85bn equity-only figure; both series rising.

corrections_to_prior_bulletins: Electronics disbursal: Rs 15,554cr (~Mar 2026, LSEM+ITHW combined, PRID 2246089) supersedes the Rs 6,834cr (Aug 2024) figure in the policy dashboard.
Auto disbursal: Rs 2,377.56cr (~Mar 2026, PRID 2246089) supersedes Rs 1,350.83cr (Dec 2025) -- payouts accelerating, grade still D+ on level.
Specialty Steel: PIB discloses Rs 48cr disbursed AND 14 of 58 Round-1 projects withdrew -- both absent from steel.gov.in's own achievement-framed pages.

The cross-scheme 'Rs 28,748cr disbursed' figure this repo previously attributed to a DPIIT Year-End Review actually comes from Parliament-answer PRID 2230621 (20 Feb 2026); the Year-End Review carries older Jun-2025 vintage numbers.
PIB states the total program outlay inconsistently across its own releases: Rs 1.97 lakh cr (Year-End Review) vs Rs 1.91 lakh cr (PRID 2230621) -- unresolved, flagged rather than reconciled.
